name: Seared Tuna and Spinach Salad with Warm Pancetta Dressing
recipeIngredient: 1 bag spinach (10 ounces) - cleaned and stemmed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F. Season the shallots with salt and pepper.
2. Place the shallots in a small shallow baking pan and cover with port wine. Roast for about 10-15 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from the oven and set aside, reserving the liquid.
3. In a sauté pan, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium heat. Add pancetta (diced) and bay leaf. Cook until crispy, about 10 minutes. Transfer to a small mixing bowl.
4. Whisk in pommage oil, rice wine vinegar, reserved shallot liquid, roasted shallots, garlic, parsley, and haricots verts. Season with salt and pepper. Set aside in a warm area on the stove.
5. Season tuna with salt and cracked black pepper. In a sauté pan, heat remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil over medium-high heat. Sear tuna for 2-3 minutes per side for rare.
6. Toss spinach with dressing. Season with salt and pepper. Place greens in center of plate. Slice tuna loins and arrange against the greens. Garnish with parsley, edible flowers, and sprouts.
7. Yield: 4 servings
